food_name: Almond Butter, Natural (Plain Roasted Almonds, No Added Salt)
region_of_origin: United States (California); almonds originate from Mediterranean region and Central Asia
food_subgroup: Nut butters — natural almond butter (plain roasted almonds ground, no additives, no added salt)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI for natural almond butter. Closest parent concept: nut butter (almond). FoodOn URI not assigned; gap noted.
ontology.foodex2_description: Natural almond butter (plain, no salt added)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0ANB used as closest parent for almond butter; exact code for unsalted natural almond butter not confirmed in published FoodEx2 catalogue — treat as analogous anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for natural almond butter as of 2026.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard clinical serving of natural almond butter is 2 tablespoons (approximately 32g), providing approximately 197 kcal, 6.7g protein, 17.8g fat, and 6.0g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: Almond Butter, Plain, Without Salt Added — USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 168587
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FDC ID 168587 is an exact match for almond butter, plain, without salt added. All per-100g nutrient values are sourced directly from this record.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Almonds and Almond-Based Products — University of Sydney GI Database (whole almonds GI ~15); analogous estimate applied to almond butter
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for natural almond butter exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~14 is estimated by analogy with whole almonds (GI ~15, University of Sydney GI Database). Almond butter retains the same macronutrient profile as whole almonds; the grinding process does not materially alter glycaemic response. GL per 32g serving = (14 × 6.0g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 0.84, rounded to 0.9. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium (3mg/100g); high potassium (748mg/100g) and magnesium (66mg/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Natural almond butter contains only 3mg sodium per 100g — essentially negligible — while providing 748mg potassium and 66mg magnesium per 100g, both of which support vascular relaxation and blood pressure regulation; a 32g serving contributes approximately 239mg potassium with no meaningful sodium load, making it an excellent choice for hypertensive patients.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low GI (~14), negligible glycaemic load per serving (GL ~0.9), high fibre (10.3g/100g), and high Vitamin E (26.2mg/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 14 and a glycaemic load of only 0.9 per standard 32g serving, natural almond butter produces a negligible postprandial glucose response; its 10.3g dietary fibre per 100g further attenuates glycaemic excursions, and its exceptionally high Vitamin E content (26.2mg/100g) provides antioxidant protection against oxidative stress-mediated pancreatic beta-cell damage relevant to Type 2 Diabetes management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low saturated fat (4.2g/100g), high MUFA (34.8g/100g), zero trans fat, zero cholesterol, high Vitamin E
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Natural almond butter has a highly favourable lipid profile for cardiovascular disease management: saturated fat is only 4.2g/100g (approximately 2.5 times lower than peanut butter), monounsaturated fat is 34.8g/100g, trans fat and cholesterol are zero, and Vitamin E at 26.2mg/100g provides potent antioxidant protection against LDL oxidation; a 32g serving delivers approximately 11.1mg Vitamin E (~74% of the adult RDI of 15mg).

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High Vitamin E (anti-inflammatory antioxidant), low SFA, calcium content
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: A 32g serving of natural almond butter provides approximately 8.4mg Vitamin E (~56% of adult RDI), an anti-inflammatory antioxidant relevant to rheumatoid arthritis management, while its low saturated fat content (4.2g/100g) supports cardiovascular comorbidity management and its calcium content (266mg/100g) contributes to corticosteroid-related osteoporosis prevention.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Natural almond butter is a minimally processed nut butter made by grinding plain roasted almonds (Prunus dulcis) to a smooth or slightly textured paste, with no added salt, sugar, oil, or stabilisers. Per 100g (USDA FDC 168587), it provides 614 kcal, 21g protein, 55.5g total fat (4.2g saturated fat — approximately 2.5 times lower than peanut butter; 34.8g monounsaturated fat; 14.2g polyunsaturated fat; zero trans fat), 18.8g carbohydrate, 10.3g dietary fibre, and only 3mg sodium. It is exceptionally rich in Vitamin E (26.2mg/100g — a 32g serving provides approximately 56% of the adult daily RDI of 15mg), calcium (266mg/100g — approximately 6 times more than peanut butter), potassium (748mg/100g), and magnesium (66mg/100g). Its glycaemic index is approximately 14 (estimated by analogy with whole almonds, University of Sydney GI Database), placing it firmly in the low-GI category with a negligible glycaemic load of approximately 0.9 per standard 2-tablespoon (32g) serving. Taste is rich, nutty, and slightly earthy with a dense, creamy texture. Clinically, natural almond butter is suitable for Type 2 Diabetes (negligible GI/GL, high fibre, Vitamin E for beta-cell protection), hypertension (negligible sodium, high potassium and magnesium), and cardiovascular disease (low SFA, high MUFA, zero cholesterol, high Vitamin E). Available in Singapore at Cold Storage, NTUC FairPrice, RedMart, and specialty health stores. Record type: generic, per 100g reference.

confidence_reason: All per-100g nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 168587 (almond butter, plain, without salt added) — an exact match. GI estimated by analogy with whole almonds from the University of Sydney GI Database (confidence 0.9). No fabricated ontology codes used.
notes: water_g set to -1: moisture data not separately reported in the primary USDA FDC 168587 record as accessed; detailed moisture value not available from this source entry. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per USDA FDC 168587 — natural plant-derived nut butter contains no cholesterol (naturally zero). vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_d_mcg, and vitamin_b12_mcg are confirmed zero per USDA FDC 168587 — naturally zero or below detection threshold for this food. trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA FDC 168587. GI value of 14 is an analogous estimate based on whole almonds (GI ~15, University of Sydney GI Database); no direct GI measurement for almond butter was available in the database as of 2026-04-14. FoodEx2 code A0ANB used as closest parent anchor; exact FoodEx2 code for unsalted natural almond butter not confirmed in published catalogue. FoodOn URI not available for almond butter; closest parent is nut butter (almond). SNOMED CT code not identified. Polyunsaturated fat (14.2g/100g) derived from USDA FDC 168587 fatty acid data (total fat 55.5g minus SFA 4.2g minus MUFA 34.8g = approximately 16.5g; USDA reports PUFA directly as approximately 14.2g — value taken from source). Alias '杏仁酱' language tag corrected from 'en' (as in input) to 'chinese' in aliases_by_language.
